name: pptx-posters
description: Create research posters using HTML/CSS that can be exported to PDF or PPTX. Use this skill ONLY when the user explicitly requests PowerPoint/PPTX poster format. For standard research posters, use latex-posters instead. This skill provides modern web-based poster design with responsive layouts and easy visual integration.
capabilities: Generate HTML/CSS research posters with fixed 36x48 inch poster dimensions
Create AI-powered visual elements with poster-specific formatting requirements
Export posters to PDF via browser print functionality
Convert PDF posters to PowerPoint format using LibreOffice
Apply scientific poster design principles and typography hierarchy
Integrate with scientific-schematics skill for diagram generation
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: The 433 static findings are overwhelmingly false positives. The skill is a documentation-heavy tool for creating research posters. The 'external_commands' detections are legitimate documentation examples showing command-line workflows (creating directories, running Chrome headless, using LibreOffice). The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are pattern-based false positives triggered by words like 'md5sum' and 'sha' in PDF quality checking documentation. The 'system reconnaissance' findings are standard PDF analysis commands (pdfinfo, pdffonts, pdfimages) used for poster quality validation. The 'certificate/key files' detection is triggered by CSS linear-gradient syntax patterns. No actual dangerous code execution, credential access, or malicious patterns exist.
valueStatement: Transform research content into professional research posters using HTML and CSS with AI-generated visuals. Export to PDF for printing or convert to PowerPoint for conference presentations.
userTitle: Create HTML Research Posters with PPTX Export
limitations: Only use when user explicitly requests PPTX/PowerPoint format
Requires external tools (Chrome/Firefox) for PDF export
Limited to 5-6 content sections to prevent overflow
AI-generated visuals must follow strict poster readability guidelines

useCases.title: Conference poster presentation
useCases.description: Create visually appealing research posters for academic conferences with AI-generated diagrams and charts

useCases.title: Thesis defense poster
useCases.description: Design professional posters for thesis defenses with minimal text and maximum visual impact

useCases.title: Clinical trial results
useCases.description: Present clinical trial findings with clear data visualizations and professional formatting

promptTemplates.title: Basic poster creation
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants a simple research poster
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an HTML research poster about [TOPIC] with title, authors, introduction, methods, results, and conclusions sections. Generate AI visuals for each section first.

promptTemplates.title: Conference-specific poster
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs poster for specific conference
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a 36x48 inch HTML poster for [CONFERENCE_NAME] about [RESEARCH_TOPIC]. Follow the conference guidelines and include university logo space.

promptTemplates.title: Data-heavy poster
promptTemplates.scenario: User has complex data to visualize
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an HTML poster showcasing [DATA_TYPE] results. Create simple bar charts and diagrams with maximum 3-4 elements per visual, giant text labels.

promptTemplates.title: PPTX export required
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an HTML poster then export to PDF and convert to PPTX. Use the python-pptx method for direct PowerPoint creation with all visuals embedded.

bestPractices: Generate ALL visuals before creating HTML - target 60-70% visual content
Use poster format specifications in EVERY AI visual prompt (giant text, 50% whitespace)
Preview at 25% zoom to ensure readability from 6 feet away
Limit to 300-800 words total text across all sections
antiPatterns: Creating complex 7+ stage workflows - simplify to 3-4 mega-steps maximum
Using small text in generated visuals - always specify 80pt+ font sizes
Adding too many sections - maximum 5-6 sections to prevent overflow
Skipping visual generation - always create AI visuals before HTML assembly

faq.question: When should I use this skill instead of latex-posters?
faq.answer: Only use pptx-posters when user explicitly requests PowerPoint/PPTX format. For all other poster requests, use latex-posters as the default.

faq.question: Why are my generated visuals unreadable?
faq.answer: You forgot poster format requirements. Always include POSTER FORMAT with specifications for giant text (80pt+), 50% whitespace, and maximum 3-4 elements.

faq.question: How do I export to PowerPoint?
faq.answer: First export HTML to PDF via browser print, then convert using LibreOffice: libreoffice --headless --convert-to pptx poster.pdf

faq.question: What if content is cut off at edges?
faq.answer: You have too much content. Reduce to maximum 5 sections and 800 words total. Check browser preview before exporting.

faq.question: Can I edit the poster after creation?
faq.answer: Yes, edit the HTML file directly for quick changes. For major changes, regenerate visuals first then update HTML.

faq.question: What image resolution should I use?
faq.answer: Generate visuals at 300+ DPI for print quality. The HTML template scales images appropriately for the 36x48 inch format.
